video.title: The Man Who Sold the Eiffel Tower. Twice.
video.global_style_suffix: flat 2D cartoon explainer illustration in a Cyanide-and-Happiness style: simple minimal bodies (rounded torsos with thin noodle/stick limbs and small simple hands) but expressive, detailed faces with clear eyes, eyebrows and mouth shapes conveying real emotion; bold clean uniform black outlines, flat solid color fills, soft cell-shading with simple drop shadows and gentle directional lighting, no gradients, no photorealism, no 3D, clean vector look, simple uncluttered background
video.global_negative_prompt: photorealistic, real photograph, 3d render, caption text, paragraph text, watermark, signature, logo, blurry, grainy, deformed hands, extra fingers, extra limbs, distorted face, cluttered, messy background, oversaturated, low quality

character_bible.name: Victor Lustig
character_bible.description: a slim confident con man with a thin black mustache and a sly half-smile, wearing a sharp charcoal three-piece suit, dark red tie, dark fedora and a gold pocket-watch chain
character_bible.palette: charcoal grey, cream, dark red, gold accents

character_bible.name: Andre Poisson
character_bible.description: a short round eager businessman with a bushy brown mustache and flushed pink cheeks, wearing a brown suit, a bowler hat and a monocle

character_bible.name: Al Capone
character_bible.description: a heavyset intimidating cartoon mob boss with a wide jaw and a fat cigar, wearing a white fedora and a cream pinstripe suit with a red carnation

scenes.narration: Paris. 1925.
scenes.image_prompt: Wide establishing shot of the Eiffel Tower rising over the Paris skyline at golden hour, dramatic warm clouds, a flock of birds scattering past the tower
scenes.motion_prompt: the clouds drift slowly, the birds flap across the sky, the camera holds steady

scenes.narration: A man in a very expensive suit just sold the Eiffel Tower.
scenes.image_prompt: A confident con man stands at the base of the Eiffel Tower proudly hanging a huge blank price tag on one of its iron legs, smug grin, chest puffed out
scenes.motion_prompt: the giant price tag swings gently on its string, his coat flutters slightly in the breeze

scenes.narration: For scrap metal.
scenes.image_prompt: A huge pile of rusty iron beams, bolts and girders stacked on the ground, a small blank paper tag sitting on top of the heap

scenes.narration: The buyer handed over a briefcase full of cash.
scenes.image_prompt: An open leather briefcase overflowing with thick stacks of green banknotes being slid across a polished wooden table by two eager hands

scenes.narration: For a tower he did not own.
scenes.image_prompt: A short round businessman lovingly hugging a tiny souvenir model of the Eiffel Tower, cartoon hearts floating around his head, eyes closed in bliss

scenes.narration: And when nobody came to arrest him,
scenes.image_prompt: The con man peeking slyly over the top of an open newspaper in a Parisian cafe, one eyebrow raised, a tiny espresso cup on the table

scenes.narration: he came back,
scenes.image_prompt: A vintage steam train racing toward the city at dusk, thick smoke trailing behind, the silhouette of the Eiffel Tower on the horizon

scenes.narration: and he sold it again.
scenes.image_prompt: The con man hanging a second huge blank price tag on the Eiffel Tower while winking at the viewer, green banknotes fluttering down around him like confetti
scenes.motion_prompt: banknotes flutter down slowly around him like falling leaves, he holds his wink

scenes.narration: This is the story of the greatest con man who ever lived.
scenes.image_prompt: A dramatic portrait of the con man tipping his fedora inside an ornate golden picture frame, a single warm spotlight from above, dark elegant background
scenes.motion_prompt: he slowly tips his fedora, the spotlight glows softly

scenes.narration: His name was Victor Lustig. And his favorite weapon was the morning newspaper.
scenes.image_prompt: The con man relaxing in a leather armchair calmly reading a large newspaper, steam curling up from a coffee cup on a side table

scenes.narration: See, in 1925, Paris could barely afford to maintain the Eiffel Tower. It was rusting.
scenes.image_prompt: The Eiffel Tower drawn with sad droopy cartoon eyes, patches of orange rust on its iron legs, a few bolts popping loose, one tiny worried maintenance worker looking up from below

scenes.narration: So Lustig printed himself fake government stationery,
scenes.image_prompt: Close-up of white-gloved hands holding an official-looking blank document with a red wax seal and elegant flourishes, a vintage printing press looming in the background

scenes.narration: booked the fanciest hotel in Paris,
scenes.image_prompt: The grand facade of a luxurious Parisian hotel with gold trim, red awnings and a doorman bowing deeply at the entrance

scenes.narration: and invited the five biggest scrap metal dealers in the city to a very secret meeting.
scenes.image_prompt: Five nervous businessmen in suits seated around a long table in an opulent hotel suite, the con man standing at the head of the table with his arms spread wide in welcome

scenes.narration: The government, he whispered, was secretly selling the Eiffel Tower for scrap.
scenes.image_prompt: The con man leaning in close with one hand cupped beside his mouth, five businessmen leaning toward him with huge shocked eyes and open mouths

scenes.narration: One dealer, Andre Poisson, wanted the deal so badly, he even slipped Lustig a bribe on top.
scenes.image_prompt: A short round businessman sweating with excitement while stuffing bundles of cash into the con man's open hands under a table, the con man looking away innocently

scenes.narration: Lustig cashed everything, and vanished to Vienna.
scenes.image_prompt: The con man sprinting onto a departing steam train clutching an overstuffed suitcase, loose banknotes trailing behind him in the wind

scenes.narration: And Poisson? He was so embarrassed, he never even told the police.
scenes.image_prompt: The short round businessman standing alone and tiny beneath the enormous Eiffel Tower, blushing bright red, holding a single worthless piece of paper that droops in his hand

scenes.narration: So six months later, Lustig strolled right back into Paris, and ran the exact same con again.
scenes.image_prompt: The con man strolling confidently across a Paris square twirling a cane, the Eiffel Tower behind him, a few pigeons scattering out of his way

scenes.narration: He fled to America, where he sold a magic wooden box that printed perfect hundred dollar bills.
scenes.image_prompt: A polished wooden box with brass dials and a hand crank slowly pushing out a single crisp green banknote, little sparkles around it, sitting on a deep red velvet cloth
scenes.motion_prompt: the crank turns slowly, the banknote slides out smoothly, the sparkles twinkle

scenes.narration: The box printed exactly two bills. Both real. He had loaded them inside himself.
scenes.image_prompt: A cutaway diagram of the wooden money box revealing just two real banknotes hidden in a secret inner slot, a bold arrow pointing at the empty machinery inside

scenes.narration: He even conned Al Capone, and walked away with a five thousand dollar reward for his honesty.
scenes.image_prompt: A heavyset mob boss behind a huge oak desk laughing warmly while handing cash to the calm smiling con man, two bodyguards glowering in the shadows behind them
scenes.motion_prompt: cigar smoke drifts upward, the mob boss chuckles gently

scenes.narration: But in 1935, the Secret Service finally caught him. Counterfeiting, millions.
scenes.image_prompt: Two stern federal agents in trench coats snapping handcuffs onto the con man's wrists, a stack of counterfeit printing plates on the table beside them, his fedora tilted

scenes.narration: The day before his trial, he cut up his bedsheets, and climbed out of a New York jail window.
scenes.image_prompt: The con man calmly climbing down the outside wall of a tall jail building on a rope of knotted white bedsheets at dusk, completely unbothered
scenes.motion_prompt: the bedsheet rope sways gently as he slowly descends

scenes.narration: And pretended to be a window washer. He even waved at the crowd below.
scenes.image_prompt: The con man dangling on the bedsheet rope cheerfully wiping a jail window with a rag while waving down at a small confused crowd pointing up at him

scenes.narration: They sent him to Alcatraz. The one prison you cannot talk your way out of.
scenes.image_prompt: The grim silhouette of Alcatraz island prison on its lonely rock in cold grey fog, seagulls circling above, small dark waves below
scenes.motion_prompt: the fog drifts slowly, seagulls circle, the waves lap gently

scenes.narration: He died in prison in 1947.
scenes.image_prompt: An empty prison cell with a neatly made bed, a single dark fedora resting on the pillow, one soft shaft of light falling through the barred window

scenes.narration: And on his death certificate, under occupation, someone wrote... apprentice salesman.
scenes.image_prompt: Extreme close-up of a fountain pen finishing a single flowing line on an official cream document beside a round government stamp, warm lamplight, shallow simple background
